must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume explicitly lists GitHub and Bitbucket under tools and mentions 'Managed version control using GitHub' at CGI.
Jenkins is listed as a DevOps tool and used in the PMI project for CI/CD pipelines, but strictly for Salesforce deployments, not Python back-end services.
Postman is listed in tools, implying API interaction, but no evidence of building or designing RESTful APIs or working with JSON programmatically.

reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a 7-year Salesforce Release and DevOps Specialist with zero evidence of Python development, back-end frameworks, databases (MySQL/PostgreSQL/MongoDB), or unit testing with PyTest. The JD requires a Junior Python Developer who can build back-end services, write Django/Flask/FastAPI code, and — per recruiter notes — has Python projects and AI model training experience. Vaishnavi's strongest signals (Copado mastery, Salesforce CI/CD, 7 years at Technology Lead level) are entirely irrelevant to this role. The only marginal overlap is Git/Bitbucket version control and peripheral API exposure via Postman. Do not proceed — this is a domain mismatch, not a level mismatch.
strengths: SALESFORCE DEVOPS MASTERY: 7 years of hands-on CI/CD automation using Copado, Flosum, and Jenkins across major clients including Apple and Philip Morris International — but this is entirely irrelevant to a Python back-end developer role.
GIT/VERSION CONTROL: Demonstrated use of GitHub and Bitbucket across multiple enterprise projects at Infosys and CGI, which is the only transferable skill to the JD requirements.

CERTIFICATIONS DEPTH: Holds 13+ Salesforce and DevOps certifications including Copado Mentor-level recognition — impressive pedigree, but exclusively in a domain unrelated to this Python Developer role.
gaps: NO PYTHON SKILLS WHATSOEVER: The JD's core requirement is Python (3.x) with frameworks like Django, Flask, or FastAPI. The resume contains zero mention of Python, any back-end framework, or related database work — this is an absolute disqualifier for a Python Developer role.

NO AI MODEL TRAINING OR PYTHON PROJECTS: The recruiter explicitly wants candidates who have built Python projects and trained AI models. This candidate's only AI exposure is a 'Salesforce AI Associate' certification and Agentforce — neither involves Python or ML model training, making her a complete mismatch on the recruiter's priority criteria.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated in resume; JD has no specified maximum, so no penalty applied.
Candidate is based in Bengaluru/Bangalore, matching the JD's In-office Bangalore requirement.
JD requires 0 relevant experience as minimum; no experience gap penalty applicable.
JD specifies 'Any' gender preference; no penalty applied.
No college preference or exclusion set in JD; Lovely Professional University is neither penalised nor rewarded.
